channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Co-upcycling spent lithium-ion batteries and plastics into microwave absorbing materials with Ni-Co catalyst control
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 16 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-69501-1

This study demonstrates a sustainable strategy to co-upcycle spent lithium-ion batteries and plastic waste into carbon nanotube composites with microwave absorption, enabling efficient metal recovery while reducing environmental and carbon impacts.
item.dc:title: Co-upcycling spent lithium-ion batteries and plastics into microwave absorbing materials with Ni-Co catalyst control
item.dc:creator: Baolong Qiu
Yuanzhao Hou
Zhan Shi
Pin Du
Nuo Xu
Hongwei Xie
Kuiren Liu
Jianshe Chen
Binchuan Li
Qing Han
Dihua Wang
Lawrence Yoon Suk Lee
Mengjie Liu
Daxue Fu
Huayi Yin

item.title: Molecular electronic chirality in copper phthalocyanine induced via twisted π-π stacking on bilayer graphene
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 16 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-69713-5

Electronic chirality is a fascinating but still poorly understood phenomenon. Here, the authors show that CuPc molecules on bilayer graphene exhibit electronic chirality driven by twisted π–π interactions, which can be reversibly switched using an STM tip.
item.dc:title: Molecular electronic chirality in copper phthalocyanine induced via twisted π-π stacking on bilayer graphene
item.dc:creator: Hao-Jun Qin
Rui-Jing Sun
Jia-Jun Liu
Wen-Ao Liao
Dao-Bo Wang
Jiang Yu
Tian-Hao Leng
Chao-Fei Liu
Wen-hao Zhang
Ying-Shuang Fu

item.title: An LCA-assisted hierarchical design of radiative cooling coating for full life-cycle CO<sub>2</sub> reduction
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 16 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-69560-4

Current radiative coating strategies only consider reducing CO₂ emissions in service stage. Here, the authors present a life‑cycle–based design using a carbon‑negative filler and durable resin, achieving CO₂ reductions across the entire lifespan compared to the commercial reflective cooling baseline.
item.dc:title: An LCA-assisted hierarchical design of radiative cooling coating for full life-cycle CO<sub>2</sub> reduction
item.dc:creator: Ningning Cao
Haowen Chi
Yuxin Chen
Chen Zhu
Tianji Liu
Jiabao Li
Yue Ma
